title: Dynamic Treatment on Networks

authors.name: Bengusu Nar
authors.affiliation: Booth School of Business, University of Chicago

authors.name: Jiguang Li
authors.affiliation: Booth School of Business, University of Chicago

authors.name: Veronika Rockova
authors.affiliation: Booth School of Business, University of Chicago
authors.role: Bruce Lindsay Professor of Econometrics and Statistics

authors.name: Panos Toulis
authors.affiliation: Booth School of Business, University of Chicago

abstract_truncated: We integrate network interference and dynamic treatment perspectives and propose Q-Ising, a three-stage pipeline that (i) estimates network adoption dynamics via a Bayesian dynamic Ising model from a single observed panel, (ii) augments treatment adoption histories with continuous posterior latent states, and (iii) learns a dynamic policy via offline reinforcement learning.
key_claims: Q-Ising combines Bayesian dynamic Ising inference with offline RL for sequential decision-making under network interference from a single observational trajectory
Provides finite-sample regret upper bound decomposing into offline-RL uncertainty, network abstraction error, and first-stage Ising estimation error
Greedy (myopic) policies are provably suboptimal under network interference
Q-Ising matches or outperforms centrality-based baselines and plain DQN on SBM and real Indian microfinance village networks
Ensemble posterior sampling via MCMC provides interpretable uncertainty quantification over dynamic treatment decisions
manual_review_items: EMVS inner-loop solver (confidence 0.62) — verify against Rockova & George (2014)
Village pipeline is a partial stub — needs data download + full pipeline body
LIR baseline formula is a proxy — verify against Liu et al. (2017)
